item.title: City win League Cup as O'Reilly sinks Arsenal after Kepa blunder
item.description: Manchester City punished Kepa Arrizabalaga's howler to lift the League Cup as Nico O'Reilly's double sealed a 2-0 win against Arsenal in Sunday's final at Wembley....

item.title: City win League Cup as O'Reilly sinks Arsenal after Kepa blunder
item.description: Manchester City punished Kepa Arrizabalaga's howler to lift the League Cup as Nico O'Reilly's double sealed a 2-0 win against Arsenal in Sunday's final at Wembley....

item.title: Inter's Serie A lead cut to six with Fiorentina draw
item.description:
Inter Milan's lead at the top of Serie A was cut to six points on Sunday after a 1-1 draw at Fiorentina, as upstarts Como boosted their hopes of Champions League football by hammering Pisa 5-0.
...

item.title: Former France and West Ham star Payet announces retirement
item.description:
Former France and West Ham attacker Dimitri Payet announced his retirement from football on Sunday during a live interview on French television.
...

item.title: Dhaka’s air ‘unhealthy for sensitive groups’ with AQI score of 134
item.description: Dhaka, the densely populated capital of Bangladesh, ranked seventh among the world’s most polluted cities on Sunday morning, recording an Air Quality Index score of 134 at about 11:00am......

item.title: Three people killed as bus ploughs through crowd in Feni
item.description: Three people were killed and at least five others were injured as a bus ploughed through a crowd at Rampur on Dhaka–Chattogram highway in Feni on early Sunday......

item.title: Milan move to within five points of Serie A leaders Inter
item.description: AC Milan closed the gap separating them from Serie A leaders Inter Milan to five points as they beat Torino 3-2 at the San Siro on Saturday....

item.title: Japan coach says Asian Cup crown 'well-deserved' for inspirational team
item.description: Japan coach Nils Nielsen hailed the character of his ‘inspirational’ side after they withstood a sustained Australian barrage to win the Women's Asian Cup, with the Greenlander calling the title well-deserved....

item.title: Spurs felled by Forest in relegation battle, Sunderland shock Newcastle
item.description: Tottenham slipped ever closer to relegation from the Premier League with a 3-0 defeat to fellow strugglers Nottingham Forest on Sunday as Sunderland stretched their hex over local rivals Newcastle....

item.title: Iranian missiles sow panic, destruction in Israeli towns
item.description: Sheltering from an Iranian missile attack on his town in southern Israel on Saturday, 17-year-old Ido Franky heard ‘terrifying’ blasts like nothing he had experienced before......

item.title: Liverpool, Chelsea slip up in Champions League race
item.description:
Chelsea failed to take advantage of another Liverpool stumble in the race to qualify for next season's Champions League on Saturday, hammered 3-0 by Everton to remain outside the top five.
...

item.title: Pakistan's T20 cricket league cuts venues, crowds due to Gulf crisis
item.description: Pakistan's Twenty20 league will be held on schedule from March 26 but without crowds and only at two venues to save resources due to the Gulf crisis, the country's cricket chief announced Sunday....

item.title: South Africa clinch 19-run win over New Zealand in fourth T20
item.description: South Africa's spinners played a decisive role as the tourists won the fourth Twenty20 international against New Zealand by 19 runs on Sunday to square the five-match series 2-2....

item.title: Araujo header scrapes Liga leaders Barca win over Rayo
item.description:
Ronald Araujo's header helped Barcelona extend their lead on Real Madrid at the top of La Liga to seven points with a tight 1-0 win over Rayo Vallecano on Sunday.
...

item.title: Araujo header scrapes Liga leaders Barca win over Rayo
item.description:
Ronald Araujo's header helped Barcelona extend their lead on Real Madrid at the top of La Liga to seven points with a tight 1-0 win over Rayo Vallecano on Sunday.
...

item.title: Araujo header scrapes Liga leaders Barca win over Rayo
item.description:
Ronald Araujo's header helped Barcelona extend their lead on Real Madrid at the top of La Liga to seven points with a tight 1-0 win over Rayo Vallecano on Sunday.

item.title: Israeli settlers burn buildings in attacks on West Bank villages
item.description: The reported arson late Saturday came in the midst of a spate of killings of Palestinians by Israeli settlers in the West Bank since the start of the Middle East war......

item.title: Amusement spots in Dhaka draw huge crowds during Eid holidays
item.description: Amusement spots, parks, historic sites and other recreation centres in Bangladesh capital Dhaka drew a huge influx of visitors, especially children accompanied by their families, on Sunday, the second day of Eid-ul-Fitr......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Israeli bridge strikes ‘prelude to ground invasion’: Lebanon
item.description: ‘These attacks represent a dangerous escalation and flagrant violation of Lebanon’s sovereignty, and are considered a prelude to a ground invasion,’ Lebanese president said......

item.title: Cox’s Bazar beach bustles with tourists during Eid holidays
item.description: Cox’s Bazar sea beach has transformed into a vibrant mosaic of life and celebration, as waves of tourists pour in from morning till dusk—breathing fresh energy into every stretch of the shoreline and turning the entire coastline into a lively festival of joy on Sunday......

item.title: Trump gives Iran 48 hours to open Hormuz as Tehran strikes Israel
item.description: US president Donald Trump on Saturday gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to shipping or face the destruction of its energy infrastructure, as Tehran launched its most destructive attack yet on Israel......

item.title: 12 killed as train hits bus on Cumilla rail crossing
item.description: At least 12 people were killed and 20 others were injured as a train hit a bus on a rail crossing in the Paduar Bazar area under Sadar Dakhkhin upazila in Cumilla district early Sunday......

item.title: 25 ships unload fuel at Chattogram port in 22 days of March
item.description: Despite the global unrest due to the war between the USA-Israel and Iran, fuel unloading activities at Chattogram Port remain unaffected as a total of 25 ships have successfully unloaded fuel at the port between March 1 and March 22......

item.title: Banned BCL brings out flash procession at DU
item.description: Witnesses and university officials said that a group of about 18–20 people brought out the procession near the Raju Memorial Sculpture and its adjacent intersection......

item.title: Fuel crisis persists as long queues grip filling stations in Dhaka
item.description: Despite expectations that the Eid holidays would ease the ongoing fuel crisis, the second day of Eid-ul-Fitr saw even longer queues of vehicles at the few operational filling stations in the capital.......

item.title: Missile strike by Iran erodes sense of security in Israel’s nuclear town
item.description: Galit Amir said she thought the presence of a key Israeli nuclear facility on the edge of her hometown Dimona meant it would be well-protected by air defences during the war with Iran....

item.title: Two arrested with foreign pistol, ammunition in Dhaka
item.description: The police arrested two people along with a foreign pistol, ammunition and a private car during a raid in the capital’s Khilgaon area on Sunday morning......

item.title: Resolution in US House seeks recognition of 1971 Bangladesh genocide
item.description: A resolution introduced in the US House of Representatives calls for formal recognition of the 1971 Bangladesh genocide, bringing renewed attention to atrocities committed by Pakistani forces......

item.title: G7 wants ‘immediate and unconditional’ halt to Iran attacks: statement
item.description: Top envoys for the Group of Seven advanced economies and the European Union on Saturday urged an ‘immediate and unconditional’ end to Iran attacks against allies in the Middle East......

item.title: DB arrests man for posing as PM’s adviser
item.description: During primary interrogation and analysis of his mobile phone, police found that he introduced himself as a special adviser to the Prime Minister’s Office. He allegedly used the rubberstamp of PMO director Shahabuddin Jamil and circulated a fabricated biography of the prime minister......

item.title: Saudi Arabia expels Iran military attache
item.description: Saudi Arabia on Saturday ordered an Iranian diplomat and three members of his team to leave the country, as the kingdom and its Gulf neighbours face Iranian strikes in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks......

item.title: Doha confirms 4 Qataris, 3 Turks killed in helicopter crash
item.description: Qatar on Sunday confirmed four of its military personnel and three Turkish nationals, including one serviceman, were killed on board a helicopter that crashed in Qatar’s territorial waters......

item.title: Iran threatens Middle East infrastructure after Trump ultimatum
item.description: Iran’s defiant response came after its missiles slipped through air defences and struck two towns in southern Israel, including one housing a nuclear facility, underscoring Tehran’s continued ability to retaliate ......

item.title: PM mourns casualties in Cumilla train-bus collision
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman has expressed deep shock and sorrow over the casualties caused by a train-bus collision in Cumilla, as well as other accidents in different parts of the country, including Habiganj....

item.title: Liberation War affairs ministry working on July fighters: minister
item.description: Liberation War affairs minister Ahmed Azam Khan on Sunday said that his ministry was working not only on issues of the Liberation War but also on those who were martyred or injured in the 2024 mass uprising......

item.title: Engineer killed in Natore road accident hours before marriage
item.description: Just a day before his wedding, a 28-year-old engineer’s life came to a tragic end in a road accident in Goromati area in Natore’s Baraigram area on Sunday morning......

item.title: BNP announces two-day programme to mark Independence Day
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Sunday announced a two-day programme to observe Independence and National Day on March 26.......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Automatic signals to be introduced at all railway crossings: agri minister
item.description: Agriculture, livestock and food minister Mohammad Amin Ur Rashid has declared to introduce automatic signal systems at railway crossings passing over highways in the wake of the horrific bus-train collision in the Paduarbazar Bishwa Road area in Cumilla district......

item.title: Israel orders army to destroy more bridges in south Lebanon
item.description: Israeli defence minister Israel Katz said on Sunday that the army had been ordered to destroy more bridges over the Litani River being used by Hezbollah in south Lebanon and to step up the demolition of houses......

item.title: Saudi Arabia reports three ballistic missiles targeted Riyadh area
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s Ministry of Defense said Sunday three ballistic missiles were detected around the capital, as the kingdom and its Gulf neighbours face Iranian strikes in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks......

item.title: 64 killed in attack on Sudan hospital: WHO
item.description: A strike on a hospital in Sudan killed 64 people and wounded 89 others, the World Health Organisation said Saturday, with 13 children counted among the dead......

item.title: Two probe committees formed over Cumilla bus-train collision
item.description: Bangladesh Railway Authority has formed two separate investigation committees to investigate into the tragic train-bus crash in Cumilla that left 12 people killed......Bangladesh travel guide
